name: Romesco-Style Roasted Pepper Dip
recipeInstructions:
Preheat broiler. Line a heavy large baking sheet with foil. Arrange
peppers cut side down in center of baking sheet; surround with garlic.
Broil 6 inches from heat until peppers are blackened and garlic is tender,
turning garlic often, about 12 minutes. Gather foil up around peppers and
garlic; let stand until cool enough to handle, about 10 minutes. Working
over a bowl to catch any juices, peel and seed peppers; reserve juices.
Keeping red and yellow peppers separate, cut into pieces; transfer to 2
separate bowls. Peel and chop garlic, discarding any hard pieces.
Finely chop 6 almonds in processor. Add yellow peppers, half of garlic,
1 teaspoon reserved pepper juice, 1/2 tablespoon oil, 1/2 teaspoon vinegar
and pinch of cayenne to the processor; puree. Season with salt and pepper.
Transfer to a small bowl.
Using clean processor, repeat with remaining 6 almonds, red peppers,
remaining garlic, 1 teaspoon pepper juice, 1/2 tablespoon olive oil,
1/2 teaspoon vinegar and pinch of cayenne. Season with salt and pepper.
(Can be made up to 6 hours ahead. Cover purees separately and chill.
Bring to room temperature before serving.)
Simultaneously spoon each puree into shallow bowls. Shake the bowl
gently, allowing the purees to meet in the center. Serve with crudites and
pita wedges. Makes about 1-1/4 cups.
Per 2-tablespoon serving: calories, 30; fat, 2 g; sodium, 1 mg;
cholesterol, 0.
